title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer identifies a **stale readiness check** bug across multiple files: after updating a Kubernetes object's spec, the code checks readiness against the *pre-update* status (still in memory from the initial GET), causing false-positive "ready" results before the controller has processed the change. The fix is to either re-fetch the object after update or return `false` unconditionally on the update path, and to add an `ObservedGeneration < Generation` guard in `IsDeploymentReady`. Additionally, the reviewer flags that `EnsureService` drops auto-assigned `NodePort` values on update, and that `CreateImmutableConfigMap` doesn't verify owner references when a ConfigMap already exists.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: Added a re-fetch (c.Get) after c.Update in EnsureDeployment (deployment.go:46-49) so IsDeploymentReady evaluates fresh server-side status. The same re-fetch pattern was applied to the additional locations mentioned in this comment: EnsureDatabase (database.go:49-53), ensureUser (database.go:103-107), ensureGrant (database.go:134-137), and EnsureCertificate (tls.go:48-52).

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: Added an ObservedGeneration < Generation guard at the top of IsDeploymentReady (deployment.go:103-105) that returns false when the deployment controller has not yet processed the latest spec. Two tests were added: TestIsDeploymentReady_true sets matching Generation/ObservedGeneration, and TestIsDeploymentReady_false_observedGenerationLag verifies the guard rejects stale status.

code_comments.body:
**Stale readiness check after spec update**

After `c.Update(ctx, existing)` on line 46, `IsDatabaseReady(existing)` on line 50 evaluates the status conditions that were populated during the initial `c.Get()` call (line 30) and reflect the *pre-update* state. If the Database was previously Ready and the spec just changed (e.g. `CharacterSet` update), the function returns `true` before the MariaDB operator has had a chance to react to the spec change.

Re-fetch the object after update or return `false` unconditionally on the update path to let the next reconcile cycle evaluate the fresh status.

The same issue applies to `ensureUser` (lines 94–99) and `ensureGrant` (lines 119–124).

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/database/database.go
Line: 45-50

Comment:
**Stale readiness check after spec update**

After `c.Update(ctx, existing)` on line 46, `IsDatabaseReady(existing)` on line 50 evaluates the status conditions that were populated during the initial `c.Get()` call (line 30) and reflect the *pre-update* state. If the Database was previously Ready and the spec just changed (e.g. `CharacterSet` update), the function returns `true` before the MariaDB operator has had a chance to react to the spec change.

Re-fetch the object after update or return `false` unconditionally on the update path to let the next reconcile cycle evaluate the fresh status.

The same issue applies to `ensureUser` (lines 94–99) and `ensureGrant` (lines 119–124).

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: Added a re-fetch (c.Get) after c.Update in EnsureDatabase (database.go:49-53). The same fix was applied to ensureUser (database.go:103-107) and ensureGrant (database.go:134-137) as noted in the comment.

code_comments.body:
**Stale readiness check after spec update**

After `c.Update(ctx, existing)` on line 45, `IsCertificateReady(existing)` on line 49 is evaluated against `existing.Status.Conditions` as populated by the initial `c.Get()` call (line 29). If the Certificate was previously Ready and the spec just changed (e.g. `dnsNames` update), the function returns `true` before cert-manager has acknowledged the new spec. 

Re-fetch the object after update or return `false` unconditionally on the update path to avoid false-positive readiness signals immediately after a spec change.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/tls/tls.go
Line: 44-49

Comment:
**Stale readiness check after spec update**

After `c.Update(ctx, existing)` on line 45, `IsCertificateReady(existing)` on line 49 is evaluated against `existing.Status.Conditions` as populated by the initial `c.Get()` call (line 29). If the Certificate was previously Ready and the spec just changed (e.g. `dnsNames` update), the function returns `true` before cert-manager has acknowledged the new spec. 

Re-fetch the object after update or return `false` unconditionally on the update path to avoid false-positive readiness signals immediately after a spec change.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: Added a re-fetch (c.Get) after c.Update in EnsureCertificate (tls.go:48-52) so IsCertificateReady evaluates post-update status.

code_comments.body:
**`NodePort` assignments are dropped on update**

The code preserves `ClusterIP`/`ClusterIPs` (lines 71–72) but does not preserve `NodePort` values that the API server assigns to `NodePort`-type Services. If the desired `svc` spec contains `NodePort: 0` (omitted) and the existing Service has auto-assigned node ports, the update will reset them to zero, which will break external access via those ports.

Also preserve existing `NodePort` values per port entry, or document that this function does not support `NodePort`-type Services.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/deployment/deployment.go
Line: 70-77

Comment:
**`NodePort` assignments are dropped on update**

The code preserves `ClusterIP`/`ClusterIPs` (lines 71–72) but does not preserve `NodePort` values that the API server assigns to `NodePort`-type Services. If the desired `svc` spec contains `NodePort: 0` (omitted) and the existing Service has auto-assigned node ports, the update will reset them to zero, which will break external access via those ports.

Also preserve existing `NodePort` values per port entry, or document that this function does not support `NodePort`-type Services.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: Added NodePort preservation logic in EnsureService (deployment.go:77-89). For each desired port with NodePort == 0, the code finds the matching existing port by Port+Protocol and copies its NodePort value. Test TestEnsureService_updatesPreservingNodePorts verifies that auto-assigned NodePorts survive a spec update.

code_comments.body:
**`CreateImmutableConfigMap` does not verify the existing ConfigMap's owner reference**

When the `IsAlreadyExists` branch is taken (line 157), the function returns the name (line 158) without confirming that the already-existing ConfigMap is owned by the `owner` parameter. This is possible in two scenarios:

1. The owner resource was deleted and recreated (new UID) — GC hasn't yet removed the old ConfigMap with the old owner reference, so `IsAlreadyExists` is returned and the new owner silently adopts a ConfigMap that is still being garbage-collected.
2. A human or another controller manually created a ConfigMap with the same name.

Fetch and verify the owner reference when `AlreadyExists` is returned, or at minimum log a warning to catch accidental ConfigMap reuse.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/config/config.go
Line: 156-159

Comment:
**`CreateImmutableConfigMap` does not verify the existing ConfigMap's owner reference**

When the `IsAlreadyExists` branch is taken (line 157), the function returns the name (line 158) without confirming that the already-existing ConfigMap is owned by the `owner` parameter. This is possible in two scenarios:

1. The owner resource was deleted and recreated (new UID) — GC hasn't yet removed the old ConfigMap with the old owner reference, so `IsAlreadyExists` is returned and the new owner silently adopts a ConfigMap that is still being garbage-collected.
2. A human or another controller manually created a ConfigMap with the same name.

Fetch and verify the owner reference when `AlreadyExists` is returned, or at minimum log a warning to catch accidental ConfigMap reuse.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: In CreateImmutableConfigMap (config.go:158-170), the AlreadyExists branch now fetches the existing ConfigMap, extracts its controller owner reference via metav1.GetControllerOf, and returns an error if the UID does not match the expected owner. Test TestCreateImmutableConfigMap_rejectsUnownedExisting verifies that a ConfigMap owned by a different controller is rejected.

resolution_summary: All six review comments were addressed. The stale-readiness-check pattern (comments 0, 2, 3) was fixed by adding a re-fetch via c.Get() after every c.Update() call across deployment.go (EnsureDeployment), database.go (EnsureDatabase, ensureUser, ensureGrant), and tls.go (EnsureCertificate). The ObservedGeneration guard (comment 1) was added to IsDeploymentReady. NodePort preservation (comment 4) was implemented in EnsureService by matching ports on Port+Protocol and carrying forward existing NodePort values. Owner-reference verification (comment 5) was added to CreateImmutableConfigMap's AlreadyExists branch. New tests were added for the ObservedGeneration guard, NodePort preservation, and unowned-ConfigMap rejection.
